mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 8.0 percent, labor market tightening accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving corporate earnings revisions upward and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:2011 unemployment peaked near 10.0 percent in late 2009
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 8.0 percent, or the signal's predictive power vanishes after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with expanding retail sales indicates synchronized supply-demand recovery, which boosts corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising INDPRO and RSAFS fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of observations, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid monetary tightening, rising short-term rates compress equity multiples and increase borrowing costs, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of tightening episodes, or the coefficient on FEDFUNDS changes becomes statistically indistinguishable from zero during high-inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in housing starts exceeding 5 percent signals weakening consumer confidence and construction activity, which drags down related corporate earnings and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST monthly change falls below -5.0 percent
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in more than 60% of months where HOUST drops by over 5 percent, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% in a 10-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between strong industrial output and stagnant employment indicates rising labor productivity, which expands corporate margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The ratio of INDPRO growth to PAYEMS growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of cases, or the interaction term's t-statistic remains below 1.5 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP per quarter reduces recession probabilities and supports earnings compounding, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of other macro conditions.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative in more than 55% of quarters where GDPC1 expands, or the rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion where unemployment remains below 6.0 percent, persistent labor shortages force margin compression and trigger Fed hawkishness, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of late-cycle episodes with UNRATE below 6.0 percent, or the signal's predictive power disappears when controlling for CPIAUCSL momentum.
